sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this title

special_mechanics.name: Skitching
special_mechanics.description: Primary game mechanic: player clutches onto a moving vehicle's bumper to gain momentum. Can propel off bumper to 'slingshot' to another vehicle or escape opponents. Skitching on police cars rewards more money but risks apprehension and fines.

special_mechanics.name: Jump and Tricks
special_mechanics.description: Player can jump and perform aerial tricks off ramps by pressing buttons mid-air. Successfully performed tricks are ranked by judges.

special_mechanics.name: Combat
special_mechanics.description: Unarmed and armed combat with other skaters. Weapons include nunchaku, chains, pipes, crowbars, whips, and baseball bats. Player can carry multiple weapons simultaneously.

special_mechanics.name: Vehicle Interaction
special_mechanics.description: Cars become faster and more difficult to skitch on with each race. Opponents become more aggressive as tournament progresses.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Game documentation available through Wikipedia entry. No precise physics values (velocities, accelerations, gravity constants) are documented in available sources. Gameplay mechanics are well-described qualitatively but lack quantitative movement parameters typical of Tier 1 analysis. Engine reused from Road Rash series but specific physics values not provided.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Skitchin' article (gameplay mechanics, development, features)
